titleStmt.title: SII 25.260: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 260. (A.R. No. 260 of 1908.) KURUSPĀL, BASTAR STATE, CENTRAL PROVINCES.

summary.p: On a slab in a field in this village.

This Sanskrit record in Nāgarī script may palaeographically be assigned to 11th century and thus seems to be ancient than the record from Nārāyaṇpāl dated Śaka 1033 (See, Ep. Ind., Vol. IX, pp. 311 ff and plate).

It records the grant of land situated near Kalēmva, free from all encumbrances and taxes to the deity Kāmēśvaradēva by Dhāraṇa-mahādēvī, queen of King Sōmēśvaradēva, who assumed the epithet Rājabhūshaṇa. Since the grant lands by the above queen mentioned in this place and dated in the reign period of the same king are from one and the same village Kadamba it is possible that these two records are not far away from date. The latter record from Kuruspāl is dated in the cyclic year Saumya falling in Śaka 991. Perhaps, our record may be placed around this date, if not earlier, on the basis of the grant details.
(Published in Ep. Ind., Vol. X, p. 34, Text B).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
mahāmaheśvara caraṇa kuñja
kiñjalka puṁja piñjarita
bhramarāmarāyamāṇa
śrīmadrājahabhūṣaṇa mahā
rājādhirāja śrīmatsome
śvaradeva pādānukalyāṇa vija
tharājye . svasti gaṁgā jala dhauta
jagadekamātā rāṇī dhāraṇa
mahādevi dharmmadakṣa kalemva
samipasthā Read ‘samīpasyā’bhūmiḥ pradataṁ Read ‘pradattāṁ’de
va śrī nāmeśvarasya Read ‘kāmeśvarasya’sarvva
bādhā Read ‘bādhā’parihareṇa Akare
ṇa . jasya Read ‘yasya’yasya jadā Read ‘yadā’bhūmi Read ‘yadā’
tasya tasya pālayati Read ‘yadā’jo Read ‘yah’A
nyathā karoti tasya gardabhaḥ
pitā sukarī Read ‘sūkarī’mātā .
Digital edition of SII 25.260 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
